architecture.summary: Graph-MCTS enhances LLM reasoning by using Monte Carlo Tree Search to guide structured exploration of graph-based knowledge. The system treats graph traversal as a search problem, where MCTS explores connections between entities (papers, authors, citations) and incorporates discovered knowledge into the LLM's reasoning loop. Information flows from the external graph through MCTS-directed retrieval, with results returned as text context to the LLM for token generation.

biblio.title: Monte Carlo Tree Search for Graph Reasoning in Large Language Model Agents

claims.statement: Graph-MCTS consistently outperforms existing augmentation methods across multiple LLM architectures

Most existing RAG approaches focus solely on textual data and neglect connections between pieces of knowledge, which are important in domains such as scientific publishing where entities like papers, authors, and citations form rich graphs
Meaning in graph-structured domains emerges not only from individual texts but also from their relationships
